lines.text: Central Perk, Rachel, Phoebe, Joey, Ross, and Monica are all there as Chandler enters wearing glasses.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you guys!

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: About what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you kidding? Okay, I'll give you a hint; I'll give you a hint. (Points to his glasses.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Eyes! No, no. Your eyes! No. Chandler's eyes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got glasses!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you-you've always had glasses.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No I didn't!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, did-didn't you use to have a pair? They were really round, burgundy, and they made you look kind of umm...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Feminine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sweetie, I think the glasses look great. They make you look really sexy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sitting down on the arm of her chair) You didn't think I used to wear glasses, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course! (She mouths, "I have no idea," to the rest of the gang.)
Opening Credits
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, and Monica are making some sandwiches.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So what do you guys want for an engagement present?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's okay Pheebs, we're not having a party or anything, so you don't have to get us...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupts him) If someone wants to give us a present, we don't want to deprive them of that joy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y'know what you should get 'em? One of those little uh, portable CD players.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I already have one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not unless someone borrowed it and left it at the gynecologist.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and-and-and by someone, she means Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, I know I what I want!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What we want honey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, you don't want this. I want to have your grandmother's cookie recipe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You mean the chocolate chip cookie recipe?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You mean the one that my grandmother made me swear on her deathbed that I would never let out of our family?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dying people say the craziest things.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wanted it for years! I was gonna make cookies for my children.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Break my heart-Oh, all right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I'm gonna be the mom that makes the world's best chocolate chip cookies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Our kids are gonna be fat aren't they.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Ahoy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! How's the boat?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great! I'm finally getting into this sailing stuff.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, so you finally took it out of the marina huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why would I do that? It took three guys to get the thing in there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If you don't sail your boat, what do you do on it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it's great! It's a great place to just kinda, sit, hang around, drink a few beers, eat some chips. (He says that as he sits, hangs around, drinks a beer, and eats a chip.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's good that you finally have a place to do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know Joey, I could teach you to sail if you want.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You could?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I've been sailing my whole life. When I was fifteen my dad bought me my own boat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your own boat?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! What?! He was trying to cheer me up! My pony was sick.
Central Perk, Chandler and Monica are there. He takes off his glasses and starts chewing on the ear piece.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you know what I was thinkin'?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing, I just like to go like this. (Does it again.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering from the bathrooms) Hey Chandler, what are you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh why, do you have a lecture?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then free as a bird. What's up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My dad wanted to know if you wanted to play racquetball with us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! That's great! Dad must really like you, he doesn't ask just anyone to play.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah and he didn't really ask for you, he asked for Chancy, I assumed he meant you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, did-did you correct him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I thought it would be more fun this way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so cool, maybe this is something you can do every week.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Or you can sit with him on the front porch and make sure no one steals the trash cans. He does that every week too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, just so you know, you-you have to let him win.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He hates to lose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no problem, maybe I'll play with my left hand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're not a lefty?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does anybody know me?!
Phoebe enters, walks up to Monica, and exhales exasperatedly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's wrong Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just went to my old apartment to get you the-the cookie recipe and the stupid fire burned it up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!! Why didn't you make a copy and-and keep it in a fireproof box and keep it at least a hundred yards from the original?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pauses as he figures out how to answer that) Because I'm normal! That was the one legacy my grandmother left me, and I know you wanted it as an engagement present.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, we have to get you an engagement present?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't worry about it Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No one got me an engagement present.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, here I wish you health and happiness. (She hands Monica a cookie in a plastic baggie.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: An old cookie?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) This is what happens when you don't register for gifts!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See no-no, I made a batch and I froze it, and this is the only one left.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can't accept this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 'Cause it's gross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Wait! I think I can figure out the recipe from this cookie! I do stuff like this at work all the time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! I bet I can do it.
Chandler looks over and sees Ross glaring at them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, we owe you a present.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Two! I've been engaged twice!

The Mr. Bowmont (Joey's boat), she's shown to be in one of the waterways around New York, but in reality she's in a sound stage on the Warner Bros. lot and we see New York from the water in some rather poor green screen shots. Rachel and Joey are on board.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (drinking a beer) Look at this clown! Just because he's got a bigger boat he thinks he can take up the whole river. (Yelling) Get out of the way jackass! (To Rachel) Who names his boat Coast Guard anyway?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is the Coast Guard.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are they doing out here? The coast's all the way over there. (Points to the coast, meanwhile there is coast behind him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, just ignore the boats all right? We're not finished with the lesson yet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I'm just gonna go over the basic points just one more time, are you ready?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Rach, not again. I got it! Okay? Let's start sailing, and I want to go over there (Points) where that boatload of girls is! (Yelling to them) Yo-ho-ho!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay. Is that what you want to do? You wanna go over and give a little shout out to the old, hot chickas? Okay, let's do that Sailor Joe. Quick question though, (grabs some of the rigging) what's this called?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, boat rope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wrong! How do you get the mainsail up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, rub it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. What do you do if I say we are coming about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'd say, come again. No-no, wait I-I-I know this one, I know this one, uh...
Rachel blasts an air horn in his ear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Time's up, now your dead.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And deaf!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you just go on and make your little jokey-jokes, but if you do not know what you are doing out at sea you will die at sea. Am I getting through to you sailor?! (She punctuates each word by slapping him on the forehead.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't just say yes! This isn't a game, Joey you can really get hurt out here. Okay, so do you want to pay attention or do you want to die?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I want to make a ship to shore call to Chandler.
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica and Phoebe are trying to determine the cookie recipe by eating small pieces.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I definitely taste nutmeg.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't? (Laughs) Well, that's the difference between a professional and a layman.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That and arrogance.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! How was sailing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't want to talk about it. Y'know, you could've at least saved me a whole cookie. (He grabs what's left of the cookie and pops in his mouth.)

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.text: No-wait-no-no!!!!!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (recoils in horror) Women are mean!!! (Storms out.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't believe that! Now the only thing left of my grandmother's legacy is this crumb. (She picks it up with her finger and holds it out to Monica.) I wish you a long and happy marriage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering with Chandler) Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! How was it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I had a great time! Umm, Chancy on the other hand...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I will tell the story! It was going great. I let him win. We were bonding. He even said I could call him dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And what did he ask you not to call him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Daddy. All right look, here's the story. (Flashback to Chandler about to enter the steam room as he does the voice-over.) Well, we had just finished playing racquetball and we were gonna take a steam. I walk into the steam room and it was really steamy. (The flashback shows his glasses fog up and him trying to find his way around the steam filled room. He takes off his glasses.) So I take off my glasses and that's when in happened.
[Cut to the flashback, Chandler's no longer doing the voice-over.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Over here. (You can see Ross sitting at the far wall.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Have a seat son. (You can see Mr. Geller sitting closer to the door as Chandler walks over and sits in his lap.) Hey!! (Chandler quickly jumps off and sits next to him with a shocked expression on his face.)
Commercial Break
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler is finished telling everyone what happened.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God Chandler! I can't believe it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You gave my father a lap dance!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why do they put so much steam in there?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'Cause otherwise they'd have to call it the room room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why? Okay? Why? Wh-wh-why did that have to happen?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, it's not that big a deal!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not that big a deal? There...there was touching of things.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, I know you wanted to bond with my dad, but did you really have to bond to that part?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen, I'm sure that dad doesn't care. He probably thought this was funny; he'll be telling this story for years!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't want him to tell this story for years.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, but he will. He still tells the story how Monica tried to escape from fat camp.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wasn't escaping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then how did you get caught in the barbed wire?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was trying to help out a squirrel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were trying to eat it!
The phone rings.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If that is your father calling to tell this story then the marriage is off!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (takes the phone from Ross) Come on. (Answering phone) Hello? (Listens) I'm sorry you have the wrong number. (Listens) (Whispering) Okay, I'll call you later dad. I love you. (Hangs up.)
Central Perk, Chandler, Ross, and Rachel are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (getting up) All right, I'm off to see your dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, aren't you a little over dressed?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughing) Yeah, and-and you better make sure he tips you this time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I figured I would try to convince him not to tell the story anymore, and I figure the best way to do that is face to face-And by face I don't mean his lap. And by face, I don't mean my ass. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) Hey are you getting Monica and Chandler an engagement present?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know. Y'know, they didn't get us anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well hello! So, when are we gettin' back out on the water matey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, I don't know the boat way to say this, but uh never!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because! You're mean on the boat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? I was just trying to teach you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, lesson learned! Rachel is mean!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeeeeeep... Yep-yep-yep-yep-yep. I remember when she took out on her dad's boat she wouldn't let me help at all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me, I wanted you to help, but you couldn't move your arms because you were wearing three life jackets.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You have to respect the sea! (Storms off.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look Joey, I'm sorry if-if you thought that was mean, but I gotta tell ya something. That was not mean. Okay, my father is mean. He used to yell at me all the time on the boat, I mean it was horrible. I was just being a good teacher.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does a good teacher say, "Put down the beer pinhead!?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, does a good student drink seven beers during his first lesson?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Six and a half! You knocked that last one out of my hand! Remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I didn't want you to get hit by the boom!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well it hit me anyway! And it would've hurt a lot less if I had finished that last beer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, y'know what? I-I'm sorry. I will try to tone it down and uh stop yelling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You won't boss me around anymore?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I won't boss you around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you'll be nice?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And, I'll be nice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you'll be topless?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And-Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you want me to learn?!
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is trying out different cookie recipes. Ross and Phoebe are the tasting group.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, here's batch 22. Ohh, maybe these'll taste a little like your grandmother's. This has a little bit of orange peel, but no nutmeg.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let's give it a shot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Man, I have not made this many cookies since I was in the ninth grade.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, what was that for? Like a bake sale?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, just a Friday night.
They all take a bite.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, these are pretty good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but not as good as batch 17.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Which one was that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The ones we had right after you almost threw up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah! Batch 17 was good. I did not like batch 16. (Burps a little bit.) I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are there anymore from the good batch? 'Cause we could just work off of those.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, ooh yeah, I think there is one from batch 17 left, uh... (Grabs a cookie and takes a big bite out of it and doesn't like it.) It's batch 16! 16 people! Get out of the way! (Gets up and runs for the bathroom.)
The Mr. Bowmont, its Joey's second lesson with Rachel as the resident sailing expert.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Joey honey, you're doing really good! All right, now I'm just gonna need you to step to the port side. (Joey pauses as he tries to remember which side is the port side.) Remember? Remember how we talked about the port side?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's left sweetie, but that's okay sweetie, that's a tough one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know why you just don't say left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, go to the left. (Joey goes starboard or sits on the right side of the boat.) The left!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (yelling) Just sit over there!! (Points to the port side.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (hurrying over) Okay! Okay, you're yelling again! See that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No-no, no-no-no, very quiet, said with love, no yelling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y'know what? Since I'm here, I think I'm gonna have me a little beer on the port side. (Grabs and opens one.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Joey, we're luffing a little bit, so could you tighten up the cunningham? (The mainsail has started to flap in the wind and has stopped working efficiently; she wants him to tighten it so that it starts working again.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, wow, you just said a bunch of stuff I didn't know there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, come on! We just went over this! (She does it instead.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y'know, when we did that was when that bird was flying overhead with the fish in his mouth. Did you see it? It was gross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (angrily) No! All right?! I did not see the bird! I did not see the fish! I did not see the piece of Styrofoam that was shaped like Mike Tyson! I did not, because I was trying to teach you how to sail a boat! Which obviously is an impossible thing to do!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right that's it! You're yelling and I don't see you taking your top off! I quit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What do you mean you quit?! You can't quit!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because you're not finished yet and I won't have it! Greens do not quit!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Greens? I'm a Tribbiani! And Tribbianis quit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, wait did I-I just said Greens don't quit didn't I? (Pause) (Angrily) Did I just say Greens don't quit?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Yes! You did and you're still yelling at me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! No! I'm not yelling at you, I'm just yelling near you. Oh God Joey, ohh I'm my father. Oh my God, this is horrible! I've been trying so hard not to be my mother I did not see this comin'. Oh, Joey, I'm sorry. I'm so sorry. I just wanted you to learn.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, hey I did learn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Come on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awww...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it's okay. I know what a mainsail is. (Points to it. It's the larger sail.) I know, I know to duck when the boom comes across. I-I know port is right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Left.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damnit!
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, the cookie trying period has pasted. Monica, Phoebe, and Ross are reflecting on the day's events.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, I bet it would actually make my grandmother very happy to know that we're trying to figure out her recipe. I bet she's l-l-lookin' up at us and smiling right now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Looking up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah-No, she was really nice to me, but she's in hell for sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I've tried everything. I give up. I guess I'm not gonna be the mom who makes the world's best chocolate cookies. I do make the best duck confit with broccoli rabe.  Kids love that right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww, Pheeb, come on isn't there any relative that would have the recipe? What about, what about your sister?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no-no, no, I made a promise to myself that the next time I would talk to Ursula would be over my dead body. And that's not happening 'til October 15th, 2032.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's the day you're gonna die? See-darnit, I've got shuffleboard that day.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's what you think.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I mean what about friends of your grandmother's? Wouldn't they have the recipe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, y'know I may have relatives in France who would know. My grandmother said she got the recipe from her grandmother, Nesele Tolouse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What was her name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nesele Toulouse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nestle Tollhouse?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you Americans always butcher the French language.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (grabbing a bag of Nestle Tollhouse chocolate chips) Phoebe, is this the recipe? (Tosses her the bag.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (reading the recipe on the back of the bag) Yes!! (Realizes.) Ohh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I cannot believe that I just spent the last two days trying to figure out the recipe and it was in my cupboard the whole time!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! You see it is stuff like this which is why (Looking down) you're burning in hell!!
The Gym, Chandler and Mr. Geller are heading for the whirlpool room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you understand, I'd feel a lot more comfortable if you didn't tell people what happened. Y'know, I'm a little...I'm a little embarrassed about it.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I understand completely, there's nothing more horrifying than embarrassing yourself in front of your in-laws. As a matter of fact, when I started dating Judy I was unemployed, and her father asked me what I did for a living and I told him I was a lawyer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What did you do when they found out?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: They never did, so if ever see me giving them legal advice just nod along. Shall we?

Chandler nods along and they enter the whirlpool room and remove their robes. Only Chandler is lacking a certain item of clothing. You see this is a coed whirlpool, which means swimsuits, and in fact there are two women already there and Chandler didn't seem to wear his. Needless to say, everyone is shocked, including Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So I guess we wear swimsuits in here!
Ending Credits
The Mr. Bowmont, is tied up alongside the pier; Joey and Rachel are relaxing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well Joey, I hate to admit it, your way of sailing is a lot more fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. Hey, why don't you give a pull on that rope? (Points.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh we're not sailing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just pull on it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. (She does so and it brings the cooler closer together.) Hey-hey-hey!! (Sees what's in the cooler.) Sandwiches!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What else?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (hands him one) Here you go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow! (She takes a bite, but holds the sandwich vertically so that the stuff falls out.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What you-don't hold it like that! You're lettin' all the good stuff fall out.
More falls out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh whoops.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Careful! You're wasting good pastrami! (Gasps.) Oh my God! I'm my dad!
End
